title: poivrea intermedia
intro: Poivrea intermedia, also known as the intermediate poison tree or intermediate poison ivy, is a perennial vine native to Eastern Asia, characterized by its shiny, dark green leaves with distinctive lobes and white flowers. The plant contains urushiol, a potent allergenic oil that causes skin irritation and inflammation, but it also possesses medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Poivrea intermedia include its potential to treat various skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Poivrea intermedia is typically prepared as a tincture, infusion, or ointment, and it is often used in traditional medicine in Eastern Asia, although its use should be approached with caution due to its allergenic properties.
